headline: Transcript: Down the liquidity hole
summary: Ethan Wu talks to Robert Armstrong
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Unhedged podcast episode: ‘Down the liquidity hole’

Ethan WuThe economy is slowing and yet stock prices are rising. What the hell is going on? We talked a little bit on Tuesday with Katie Martin about one idea, the classic idea that interest rate cuts are coming. The market’s pricing them in and that pushes stock prices up. But today on the show, we wanna talk about another theory of what’s going on. A weirder theory. It’s the theory of liquidity.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

This is Unhedged, the markets and finance show from the Financial Times and Pushkin. I’m reporter Ethan Wu here in the New York studio, joined today by liquidity maven Robert Armstrong.

Robert ArmstrongI think of myself as the Mobius of liquidity. Remember that — Fishburne’s character in The Matrix?

Ethan WuMorpheus. Not Mobius. Morpheus.

Robert ArmstrongWas it Morpheus? Whatever. Fishburne. That’s the character. (Ethan laughs) Long black leather trenchcoat, sunglasses. That’s me.

Etha
